heading: Reinsurance
label: 8710 Reinsurance
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large –(e).5 U.S.C. 2096(c)–(e), .Aug. 17, 1954, ch. 752, § 7(c)68 Stat. 739, .Aug. 11, 1955, ch. 794, § 369 Stat. 677The section is reorganized to clarify the steps in the computation of the insurance allocable to issuing and reinsuring companies.In subsections (c) and (d), references to the first determination date, , are omitted as executed.December 31, 1953Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1978—Subsecs. (a) to (c), (e), (f). substituted “Office of Personnel Management” and “Office” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Commission”, respectively, wherever appearing.Pub. L. 95–454
Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 95–454section 907 of Pub. L. 95–454section 1101 of this titleOct. 13, 1978

children.label: a
children.text: The Office of Personnel Management shall arrange with a company issuing a policy under this chapter for the reinsurance, under conditions approved by the Office, of portions of the total amount of insurance under the policy, determined under this section, with other life insurance companies which elect to participate in the reinsurance.

children.label: b
children.text: The Office shall determine for and in advance of a policy year which companies are eligible to participate as reinsurers and the amount of insurance under a policy which is to be allocated to the issuing company and to reinsurers. The Office shall make this determination at least every 3 years and when a participating company withdraws.
25 percent of the first $100,000,000 of the excess;
50 percent of the second $100,000,000 of the excess;
75 percent of the third $100,000,000 of the excess; and
licensed to transact life insurance under the laws of a State or the District of Columbia; and
engaged in issuing insurance certificates on the lives of employees of the United States exclusively;

children.label: e
children.text: An issuing company or reinsurer is entitled, as a minimum, to be allocated an amount of insurance under the policy equal to any reduction from , to the determination date, in the amount of the company’s group life insurance under policies issued to associations of employees of the United States. However, any increase under this subsection in the amount allocated is reduced by the amount in force on the determination date of any policy covering life insurance agreements assumed by the Office.December 31, 1953

children.label: f
children.text: The Office may modify the computations under this section as necessary to carry out the intent of this section.
